key_insights.insight: The stillest part of the hurricane is its centre, suggesting that cultivating inner stillness can provide clarity amidst life's storms.
key_insights.quote: The stillest part of the hurricane is its centre.

key_insights.insight: Welcoming and accepting whatever emotions come up, instead of pushing them away, is a critical step in the healing process.
key_insights.quote: Whatever emotion came up I didn't try and push it away or shunt it or deny it or negate it I let it I let it sit.

key_insights.insight: Reminding oneself of reality by dismantling ingrained internal models of life is essential to processing traumatic events.
key_insights.quote: I had to rip it apart. I had to take it down. I had to dismantle it.And I had to remind myself of the reality of the situation.

key_insights.insight: The mind has a mind of its own and will populate with thoughts automatically, so it is important to have a relationship with those thoughts and not judge them.
key_insights.quote: Your mind will populate your head with thoughts automatically. You're not asking for them. It'll just come up with judges, evaluations, assessments, predictions about the future, past memories, imagined scenarios.

key_insights.insight: Forgiveness, though seemingly for the other person, is ultimately a selfish act that lightens one's own emotional baggage.
key_insights.quote: Honestly, you could view it as ultimately selfish, because it's not going to impact their life, but it's going to make mine a lot easier.

key_insights.insight: Enmeshment in relationships can submerge individual identities, highlighting the importance of space for maintaining desire.
key_insights.quote: Where people find themselves in trouble is when there's enmeshment whenever there's too much when there's too much closeness when actually the identity of one person is submerged with the identity of the other and there's no space.

key_insights.insight: Living in line with one's truth is crucial for personal well-being, as neglecting it can be destructive.
key_insights.quote: When we're living in line with our truth, it saves you.And whenever we neglect our truth, it destroys you.

key_insights.insight: Deep moments of suffering can be opportunities for growth.
key_insights.quote: The deepest moments of suffering can actually be opportunities for growth.

actionable_advice.advice: Hold yourself to the highest standard during times of crisis.
actionable_advice.how_to: Choose actions you can be proud of and prioritize personal integrity over speed of recovery.

actionable_advice.advice: Embrace and welcome all emotions, seeing them as temporary experiences.
actionable_advice.how_to: Allow emotions to sit without trying to deny, negate, or push them away.

actionable_advice.advice: Ask yourself what emotional needs you are trying to meet through relationships, and find alternative ways to meet those needs.
actionable_advice.how_to: Break down ideal marriage, understand all the parts and ask can I meet those needs in a slightly different way that doesn't necessarily prescribe to the conventional model

memorable_stories: Dr. Arya recounts the moment his wife revealed her affair and pregnancy.
Stephen Bartlett shares an analogy of his breakup comparing his state of mind with terrorists in a cockpit.
Dr. Arya describes his process of forgiving his ex-wife, which involved running on the beach and repeating affirmations.
Dr. Arya bought himself a motorbike to get in touch with his love of risk and danger after being too concerned with stability and dependability.
contrarian_claims: Marriage is a collapsing system based on the decreasing marraiges, increasing divorce statistics and more people are now opting to cohabit than get married.
The belief that children need both parents in the same house.
Conventional societal scripts surrounding relationships and love should be questioned.
The aggressive alpha male is not the only way to maintain sperm on the egg and have a family line.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E56: She Cheated On Me and Thats Not All - with Dr. Aria
